index.title: Search
index.description_1: The main advantage of the system lies in allowing domain experts to directly query the archived data for simple or complex searches.
index.description_2: Both follow SPARQL logic and language, but rely on NaturalSparql technology, which suggests the possible combinations allowed by the project’s underlying semantics.
index.description_3: This makes it possible to identify potential chains starting from any entity of interest in the graph, and to select the subsequent query phrases only from the meaningful entry points.
index.access_intro: In this section you can access:
index.fast_search_description: where the entry entities have been filtered according to the thematic focuses: Investigation, Sample, Diagnostic activity, Material.
index.advanced_search_description: where the system can be queried starting from any of the entities in the knowledge graph.
index.guide_description: Practical guide to querying the system:

sistema.title: The system
sistema.description:
HD-LSD (Heritage & Diagnostics – Linked Semantic Data) is a tool for collecting and managing diagnostic data in the field of cultural heritage, within architectural, archaeological, art-historical and environmental contexts.
The consultation allows users to ask questions about data interpretation, providing workflows and best practices for both field and laboratory activities and offering documentation ranging from the macroscopic level down to the microscopic scale, exploring techniques for material characterization, production analysis, degradation identification, dating, and reconstruction of palaeoenvironments and cultivation practices.
sistema.video_description: Introduction to how the HD-LSD website works

finalita.title: Objectives
finalita.description_1: The main objective of HD-LSD is to overcome sector-based interpretation of data, opening up an interdisciplinary reading of diagnostics applied to cultural heritage and facilitating research and understanding of workflows across different fields.
finalita.description_2: Through the integration and sharing of knowledge, the platform promotes better accessibility of information for the scientific community, enhancing both field and laboratory activities.

partecipanti.title: Participants
partecipanti.intro: HD-LSD is an output of the national research project PE5 CHANGES PNRR, Spoke 5. It is part of the activities of WP6 Cross-disciplinary interpretation of data from novel diagnostics and definition of multi-method protocols for the study of Cultural Heritage, composed of an interdisciplinary team from Sapienza University of Rome involving four departments:

sdara.name: Department of History, Representation and Restoration of Architecture
sdara.people: Donatella Fiorani · Chiara Porrovecchio · Silvia Cigognetti

biologia.name: Department of Environmental Biology
biologia.people: Mauro Iberite · Laura Sadori · Alessia Masi · Eleonora Creafogli

bibliografia.title: References and bibliography
bibliografia.intro: This section collects the main scientific and documentary references used to develop the conceptual model and the theoretical framework.
bibliografia.cidoc_link_label: CIDOC CRM – Conceptual Reference Model (opens in a new window)

modelli.title: Reference models
modelli.cidoc_crm_1: The CIDOC Conceptual Reference Model (CRM) is a theoretical and practical tool for integrating information in the field of cultural heritage. It provides definitions and a formal structure for describing concepts and relationships used in cultural heritage documentation, enabling the integration of heterogeneous and distributed data independently of the software and schemas adopted.
modelli.cidoc_crm_2: CIDOC CRM promotes a shared understanding of cultural heritage information, acting as “semantic glue” between museums, archives, libraries and other information systems.
modelli.crmsci_1: The Scientific Observation Model (CRMsci) is a formal ontology designed as a global schema for integrating metadata relating to scientific observation, measurements and processed data in empirical and descriptive sciences.
modelli.crmsci_2: The model facilitates the management, integration and exchange of research data through the description of semantic relationships, particularly causal ones, in research contexts and digital infrastructures.
modelli.crminf_1: CRMinf is a formal ontology dedicated to modelling argumentation and inferential processes in descriptive and empirical sciences. It enables the structured representation of reasoning, premises, conclusions and cognitive activities.
modelli.crminf_2: Its goal is to support the integration, mediation and access to data relating to reasoning processes, promoting an explicit representation of interpretive knowledge.
modelli.crmcpm_1: The Conservation Process Model (CPM) is a formal ontology developed since 2015 by the Sapienza University of Rome research group for representing knowledge, analysis and conservation processes concerning historic architecture.
modelli.crmcpm_2: Integrated into the CIDOC CRM structure, CPM enables articulated semantic management of information relating to conservation, preserving the complexity and cultural value of historic architecture.
modelli.cidoc_link_label: CIDOC CRM – Conceptual Reference Model (opens in a new window)

crediti.title: Credits
crediti.intro: The development of HD-LSD is the result of interdisciplinary work involving scientific, methodological and IT expertise.
crediti.modeling: Design, definition and validation of conceptual and ontological models for representing diagnostic data and cultural heritage.
crediti.it_development: Design and development of the software architecture, web interfaces and services for data management and querying.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This website processes personal data exclusively for institutional and research purposes, in compliance with Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR).

controller.title: Data controller
controller.text: The data controller for personal data collected through this website is the institution responsible for the HD-LSD project, as part of its research and development activities.

data.title: Types of data processed
data.text: The website processes personal data voluntarily provided by users authorized to access the services, as well as technical data necessary for system operation.
data.item1: Identification and contact data for access to the services

purpose.title: Purposes and legal basis of processing
purpose.text: Personal data is processed exclusively to provide the services requested by the user and to ensure the security and proper operation of the system.
purpose.item1: Performance of a service requested by the user (Art. 6.1.b GDPR)
purpose.item2: Legitimate interest of the controller in system security

retention.title: Data retention
retention.text: Personal data is retained for the time strictly necessary to achieve the purposes for which it was collected and in compliance with legal obligations.

rights.title: Rights of the data subject
rights.text: Data subjects may exercise the rights provided for in Articles 15 et seq. of the GDPR, including the right of access, rectification, erasure, restriction of processing and objection.

security.title: Data security
security.text: The controller adopts appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure the security of personal data and prevent unauthorized access.

cookies.title: Cookie notice
cookies.text: The website uses only technical cookies necessary for the operation of the services. For further information, please refer to the Cookie Policy.
cookies.message: This website uses technical cookies to ensure proper operation.
cookies.more_info: More information is available in the

what.title: What cookies are
what.text: Cookies are small text files that the website sends to the user’s browser to allow pages and services to function properly.

types.title: Types of cookies used
types.text: The website uses only technical cookies necessary for browsing and accessing restricted services.
types.item2: Technical cookies for the proper operation of the website

purpose.title: Purpose of cookies
purpose.text: Technical cookies are used exclusively to enable browsing and provide the services requested by the user.

manage.title: Cookie management
manage.text: Users can configure their browser to accept or reject cookies. Disabling technical cookies may compromise the proper operation of the website.

legal.title: Legal basis
legal.text: The use of technical cookies is based on the need to provide the services requested by the user, pursuant to Art. 6.1.b of the GDPR.
